artPrompt: The dramatic moment a magician's gloved hand fans a deck toward the viewer in steep foreshortening, one blank card already sliding free trailing hot-gold sparks, the trick inevitable. Behind, a tattered carnival tent and rigged wheel glow in smoky red and sickly neon, ember motes drifting, a single drop of blood-dark sacrifice at the trickster's cuff, seductive and rigged, rendered as a bold painted graphic-novel splash — strong silhouettes, heavy directional inking and dramatic shadow shapes, fully painted (not flat cel comic).
name: Pick a Card, Any Card
text: You draw two cards and you lose 2 life. (It's the card you wanted. It was always going to be the card you wanted.)
flavorText: "Is this your card?" It is. It is always your card. That's the trick.
